title: podman build with secrets fail
body:
### Issue Description

Unable to inject a secret into the podman build process, like, at all. Anything other than an empty file fails with the following mouthful of an error:

```
PS > podman build --secret id=npm,src=.\mise.toml -t testest .
time="2025-08-27T13:32:39+02:00" level=error msg="1 error occurred:\n\t* archive/tar: write too long\n\n\n"
Error: Post "http://d/v5.6.0/libpod/build?dockerfile=%5B%22Dockerfile%22%5D&forcerm=1&httpproxy=1&idmappingoptions=%7B%22HostUIDMapping%22%3Atrue%2C%22HostGIDMapping%22%3Atrue%2C%22UIDMap%22%3A%5B%5D%2C%22GIDMap%22%3A%5B%5D%2C%22AutoUserNs%22%3Afalse%2C%22AutoUserNsOpts%22%3A%7B%22Size%22%3A0%2C%22InitialSize%22%3A0%2C%22PasswdFile%22%3A%22%22%2C%22GroupFile%22%3A%22%22%2C%22AdditionalUIDMappings%22%3Anull%2C%22AdditionalGIDMappings%22%3Anull%7D%7D&inheritannotations=1&isolation=0&jobs=1&layers=1&networkmode=0&nsoptions=%5B%7B%22Name%22%3A%22user%22%2C%22Host%22%3Atrue%2C%22Path%22%3A%22%22%7D%5D&omithistory=0&output=testest&outputformat=application%2Fvnd.oci.image.manifest.v1%2Bjson&pullpolicy=missing&retry=3&retry-delay=2s&rewritetimestamp=0&rm=1&secrets=%5B%22id%3Dnpm%2Csrc%3Dpodman-build-secret377670978%22%5D&shmsize=67108864&t=testest": io: read/write on closed pipe
```
the dockerfile is empty and mise.toml is not particularly weird:
```
PS > cat .\mise.toml
[tools]
node = "22"
```
but the issue occurs with any non-empty file. Interestingly, an empty file seems to work:
```
PS > cat .\nothing
PS > podman build --secret id=npm,src=.\nothing -t testest .
Error: building: no stages to build
```
which is the correct output given an empty dockerfile.




### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. With an empty dockerfile, run `podman build --secret id=s,src=.\<any non-empty file> -t test .`


### Describe the results you received

```
time="2025-08-27T13:42:06+02:00" level=error msg="1 error occurred:\n\t* archive/tar: write too long\n\n\n"
Error: Post "http://d/v5.6.0/libpod/build?dockerfile=%5B%22Dockerfile%22%5D&forcerm=1&httpproxy=1&idmappingoptions=%7B%22HostUIDMapping%22%3Atrue%2C%22HostGIDMapping%22%3Atrue%2C%22UIDMap%22%3A%5B%5D%2C%22GIDMap%22%3A%5B%5D%2C%22AutoUserNs%22%3Afalse%2C%22AutoUserNsOpts%22%3A%7B%22Size%22%3A0%2C%22InitialSize%22%3A0%2C%22PasswdFile%22%3A%22%22%2C%22GroupFile%22%3A%22%22%2C%22AdditionalUIDMappings%22%3Anull%2C%22AdditionalGIDMappings%22%3Anull%7D%7D&inheritannotations=1&isolation=0&jobs=1&layers=1&networkmode=0&nsoptions=%5B%7B%22Name%22%3A%22user%22%2C%22Host%22%3Atrue%2C%22Path%22%3A%22%22%7D%5D&omithistory=0&output=testest&outputformat=application%2Fvnd.oci.image.manifest.v1%2Bjson&pullpolicy=missing&retry=3&retry-delay=2s&rewritetimestamp=0&rm=1&secrets=%5B%22id%3Dnpm%2Csrc%3Dpodman-build-secret910911699%22%5D&shmsize=67108864&t=testest": io: read/write on closed pipe
PS C:\Users\peedward\projects\01_hqv\tf_vision\Frontend\react-app>
```

### Describe the results you expected

I expect podman to inform me that my dockerfile is empty. Although end game would be to use the secret while building.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes
